title: The Role of Public Transport
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Improving the (0) efficiency of public transport systems is one of the most effective ways to reduce urban congestion. Well-designed networks can significantly reduce (17) congestion and cut both air (18) pollution and carbon emissions. Passengers also benefit from lower (19) transportation costs compared to owning and running a private car.||However, many cities still struggle with (20) unreliable services and (21) insufficient funding to maintain ageing (22) infrastructure. Governments must make (23) substantial investment in public transport if they wish to create truly sustainable cities. (24) Encouraging more people to leave their cars at home could make a real difference to urban air quality and quality of life.

example.text: Improving the (0) EFFICIENCY of public transport systems is one of the most effective ways to reduce urban congestion. (EFFICIENT)

questions.explanation: The noun 'congestion' is formed from 'congest' by adding the suffix '-ion'.
The noun 'pollution' is formed from 'pollute' by replacing '-te' with '-tion'.
The noun 'transportation' is formed from 'transport' by adding the suffix '-ation'.
The adjective 'unreliable' is formed from 'rely' by changing '-y' to '-iable' and adding the prefix 'un-'.
The adjective 'insufficient' is formed from 'sufficient' by adding the prefix 'in-'.
The noun 'infrastructure' is formed from 'structure' with the prefix 'infra-' meaning 'beneath'.
The adjective 'substantial' is formed from 'substance' by replacing '-nce' with '-ntial'.
The gerund 'encouraging' is formed from 'encourage' by adding the suffix '-ing'; 'encourage' derives from 'courage'.
